onboarding.title: New Employee Onboarding
onboarding.subtitle: Complete all steps to finish your onboarding paperwork.
onboarding.completeMessage: Your forms have been submitted and sent for signing. Your employer will review your I-9 identity documents within 3 business days of your start date. You'll receive email confirmations as each document is processed.
onboarding.reviewSubtitle: Review each pre-filled form, then sign it. You can go back to edit if anything looks wrong.
onboarding.previewWarning: This is how your form will be filled. If anything is wrong, go back and correct it before signing.
onboarding.noPdfPreview: No PDF preview is available for this form. Review the details below and proceed to sign.
onboarding.signInstruction: Complete all required fields and apply your signature below.

filingStatuses.marriedJoint: Married filing jointly or Qualifying surviving spouse

citizenshipStatuses.citizen: A citizen of the United States
citizenshipStatuses.noncitizen: A noncitizen national of the United States

onboarding.completeSignatureNote: Complete your signature above to continue to the next form.
onboarding.listBCDocProvided: List B + List C documents provided

personal.title: Personal Information
personal.desc: This information is used to pre-fill your W-4, I-9, and state withholding forms. Enter it once here — you won't need to re-enter it on each form.
personal.otherLastNamesPlaceholder: Maiden name, prior last name, etc.
personal.securityNote: Your SSN and date of birth are encrypted and used only for official tax form submission. They are never stored in plain text.

addressStep.title: Home Address
addressStep.desc: Your residential address is required for W-4, I-9, and state withholding. The state you select also determines which state withholding form applies to you.

w4.title: W-4: Federal Tax Withholding
w4.filingStatusDesc: Choose the status that best describes your situation. This is the most important field and affects every paycheck.
w4.multipleJobsDesc: Check this if you (or your spouse) hold more than one job simultaneously. This prevents under-withholding.
w4.dependentsDesc: Enter the number of qualifying children under 17 (line 3a, worth $2,000 each) and other dependents (line 3b, worth $500 each). Most employees leave both at 0.
w4.otherAdjustmentsDesc: Use these fields to fine-tune withholding for investment income, itemized deductions, or extra withholding per paycheck.
w4.exemptDesc: Only claim exempt if you owed $0 in federal tax last year and expect to owe $0 this year. Most employees do not qualify.
w4.multipleJobsLabel: Multiple jobs or spouse also works (Step 2)
w4.irsNote: For the most accurate withholding, use the IRS Withholding Estimator at

stateTax.title: State Tax Withholding
stateTax.noIncomeTaxDesc: No state withholding certificate is required. Click Continue to proceed.
stateTax.instructionsDesc: State withholding varies by state. Enter your filing status and allowances as shown on your state's withholding certificate.
stateTax.allowancesExplain: Each allowance reduces the amount withheld per paycheck. Claiming more allowances = less withheld; fewer = more withheld. Enter 0 if unsure — your state will apply the default.
stateTax.exemptExplain: Only claim exempt if you owed no state income tax last year and expect none this year.

i9.title: I-9: Employment Eligibility Verification
i9.section1Title: Section 1 — Employee (Complete by your first day)
i9.section1Desc: Federal law requires you to verify your identity and authorization to work in the U.S. You complete Section 1 now; your employer completes Section 2 within
i9.section1Suffix: of your first day of employment.
i9.lprDesc: Enter your Alien Registration Number (A-Number, starts with "A") in the field provided.
i9.alienDesc: Provide your A-Number, Form I-94 Admission Number, or foreign passport details as applicable.
i9.docsHeader: Documents you must present to your employer on your first day
listAItems: Foreign Passport with I-94 or I-551 stamp
listBCItems: B: Driver's license / State ID card
i9.docUploadDesc: Pre-upload copies of your identity documents. Physical originals must be presented to your employer on or before your first day of employment.
i9.listAChoiceDesc: One document — U.S. Passport, Permanent Resident Card, or Employment Authorization
i9.listBCChoiceDesc: Two documents — Identity (e.g., Driver's license) + Employment auth (e.g., SSN card)
i9.uploadListA: Click to upload List A document (PDF or image)
i9.uploadListB: Click to upload List B document (PDF or image)
i9.uploadListC: Click to upload List C document (PDF or image)
i9.preparerToggle: A preparer or translator helped me complete this form

deposit.title: Direct Deposit Authorization
deposit.desc: Authorize payroll direct deposit to your bank account. Your routing and account numbers can be found on a check or in your bank's mobile app.
deposit.uploadVoidedCheck: Click to upload a voided check (PDF or image)

ack.title: Acknowledgments
ack.desc: Review and acknowledge the following required policies to proceed to the document signing step.
ack.workersCompBody: I acknowledge that I have been informed of my rights under the workers' compensation laws of my state. I understand that workers' compensation insurance covers work-related injuries and illnesses, and I agree to report any such injury or illness to my supervisor immediately.
ack.workersCompToggle: I acknowledge the Workers' Compensation policy
ack.handbookBody: I acknowledge that I have received, read, and understood the Employee Handbook. I understand it is not a contract of employment, that it may be updated at any time, and that I agree to comply with all policies and procedures contained within.
ack.handbookToggle: I acknowledge receipt of the Employee Handbook
ack.submitNote: After clicking Submit, you'll be guided through electronically signing your W-4, state withholding form, and I-9 via DocuSign.

errors.uploadFailed: Failed to upload document. Please try again.
errors.voidedCheckFailed: Failed to upload voided check. Please try again.
errors.previewFailed: Could not load PDF preview. Please try again.
errors.signingFailed: Could not create signing session. Please try again.

account.fontScaleHint: Adjust the text size across the application. Changes take effect immediately.
account.taxFormsIntro: Complete the following forms to finish your onboarding. Click any form to review the document and sign digitally.
account.downloadFormInstruction: Download the form, complete it, then return here to acknowledge completion.
account.digitalSigningStart: Click below to start the digital signing process.
account.signedDocAvailable: Your signed document is available for download.
account.formPendingSetup: This form is being configured by your administrator. You will be able to fill it out electronically once setup is complete.
account.acknowledgmentInstruction: Review the form above, then click below to confirm you have read and completed it.
account.identityDocsInstruction: Upload identity documents to verify your eligibility. Examples: birth certificate, driver's license, social security card, passport.
account.formAlreadySubmitted: This form has already been submitted
account.formSensitiveNote: For security, previously submitted data is not displayed. You can submit a new version if your information has changed.
account.formCompletedNote: This form was completed successfully. You can submit a new version if your information has changed.
account.docListADesc: Documents that establish both identity and employment authorization
account.fillAndSignReady: This form is ready for digital signing.
account.identityDocNeedListB: You need a document from List B (identity)
account.identityDocNeedListC: You need a document from List C (employment authorization)
account.identityDocRequired: Identity documents are required for I-9 verification
